primary_chain: New backend admitted to reserve -> sync mutates it into active / changes selected routing -> repo previously could still reach the success tail and emit a reserve-first success claim; the patch adds a post-sync verification gate that re-reads registry/state and blocks that success path.

findings.fact: Post-sync verification now re-reads registry and state and fails before the reserve-first success tail when routing or pool truth contradicts reserve-first onboarding.
New regression simulates sync promoting the selected onboarded backend to active and expects a fatal ONBOARD_ACTIVE_ROUTING_CHANGED result.
Test harness now supports registry+state sync mutation, matching the live contradiction chain instead of only the initial onboard result.

residual_risks: External onboarding/sync binaries may still attempt the mutation; this repair prevents the repo owner surface from overclaiming success afterward.
If a future sync path mutates backend identity shape instead of only pool/routing truth, another regression may be needed.
